description: Baseline: 18.4 tok/s with quick-test prompt, 100 gen tokens

asi.bottlenecks: 4x cudaDeviceSynchronize per token, 1MB logits D2H copy, CPU argmax, shared mem tree reduction in matvec

description: Warp shuffle reduction in matvec kernels + remove 3 cudaDeviceSynchronize in LM head + GPU argmax for greedy mode. Greedy: 18.4→20.5 tok/s (+11%), Sampling: 18.4→18.5 tok/s. Correctness verified.

asi.hypothesis: Replace shared memory tree reduction (7 syncthreads) with warp shuffle reduction (2 syncthreads) in all 4 quantized matvec kernels. Remove unnecessary cudaDeviceSynchronize in LM head. Add GPU argmax for greedy mode.
asi.changes: 1. block_reduce_sum template using warp shuffle 2. Removed 3 syncs in LM head 3. GPU argmax kernel (512 threads, grid-stride) 4. Greedy mode uses GPU argmax (4B D2H vs 1MB)
asi.sampling_unchanged: 18.5 tok/s - bottleneck is still 1MB D2H copy for sampling
asi.pre_existing_greedy_bug: Greedy mode produces '1000...' repeat loop after ~30 tokens - exists in baseline too
asi.next_target: Sampling mode still copies 1MB logits to CPU for top-k/top-p sampling. Need GPU sampling or at least GPU top-k to avoid copy.

description: Remove cudaStreamSynchronize from MV_PAIR. Marginal improvement 18.7 tok/s sampling.

asi.changes: Removed cudaStreamSynchronize from l_mv_pair, runs both matvecs on stream 0
asi.hypothesis: Remove sync barrier in MV_PAIR that was causing 128 unnecessary syncs per token
asi.result: Marginal improvement. The real gap is in kernel memory bandwidth efficiency, not sync overhead.

description: Batched RMS norm: fuse 48 SSM gate norms + 28 attention Q/K norms into single launches. 18.4→25.5 tok/s greedy, 18.7→24.1 tok/s sampling.

asi.changes: Fused 48 individual RMS norm kernel launches per SSM layer into single batched kernel. Also fused Q/K norm in attention layers (24+4 launches -> 2). Reduced total kernel launches from ~4659 to ~1695 per token.
asi.hypothesis: Batched RMS norm eliminates 2304+ kernel launches per token. Each launch costs ~5us overhead.
asi.correctness: Verified with greedy 100tok and sampling 200tok - output matches baseline

description: Final result: 24.5 tok/s sampling (100 tok), 24.2 tok/s (200 tok). +33% over 18.4 baseline. llama.cpp still at 41 tok/s — gap is kernel bandwidth + fused ops.

asi.cuda_graphs: +2% (smaller than expected — launch overhead not main bottleneck)
asi.gpu_argmax: Avoids 1MB D2H copy in greedy mode
asi.remaining_bottleneck: Kernel execution bandwidth (38% vs llama.cpp 62%). Matvecs achieve 70-80% in isolation but interleaved small kernels reduce overall utilization.
asi.summary: 1. Warp shuffle reduction (+11%) 2. Batched RMS norm (+29%) 3. GPU argmax 4. Q8_1 caching 5. CUDA graphs (+2%) 6. Multi-row kernels (no gain) 7. launch_bounds (no gain)
asi.warp_shuffle: Replaced 7-syncthreads tree reduction with 2-syncthreads warp shuffle in 4 matvec kernels

description: Fused Q4_K gate+up matvec kernel: single kernel computes both FFN projections sharing Q8_1 input. Marginal improvement 24.5→24.7 tok/s (0.8%). FFN is not the bottleneck.

asi.expected_gain: Fused gate+up kernel: save 1 launch + 1 Q8_1 read per FFN layer (16 layers)
asi.fused_kernel: k_q4k_gate_up_fused computes both projections in one kernel, sharing Q8_1 input
asi.hypothesis: Fusing FFN gate+up matvec into single kernel reduces launches and memory traffic
asi.next_action: Focus on SSM matvec optimization or attention kernel shared memory reduction. FFN is already fast; gains are marginal.
asi.observed: 24.7 tok/s vs ~24.5 tok/s baseline (0.8% improvement, within noise)
asi.pre_overhead: 11ms/tok in timing build - needs investigation, may include pipeline bubbles
asi.reason_for_small_gain: FFN gate+up only 2.1ms/tok total; small absolute savings
asi.ssm_bottleneck: ssmo (11.7ms) + re-g (7.5ms) = 51% of GPU time - these are the real targets

description: Reduce attention kernel shared memory from 4096 to pos+1 elements for better occupancy. No speedup - attention is not the bottleneck (~0.5ms/tok).

asi.attention_kernel: Reduced shared memory from max_ctx (4096) to kv_len (pos+1) for better occupancy on early tokens
asi.hypothesis: Smaller shared memory allocation in attention kernel improves occupancy for early tokens
asi.impact: No measurable improvement - attention is only ~0.5ms/tok, not the bottleneck
asi.next_action: Focus on SSM matvec kernels (ssmo 11.7ms, re-g 7.5ms) and FFN matvecs. Need to understand why full-pipeline matvec is 10x slower than microbenchmark.
asi.observed: 24.2 tok/s sampling (200 tok) - same as baseline
asi.pipeline_gap: Microbenchmark shows 774 GB/s for ssmo in isolation, but full pipeline shows ~70 GB/s effective. Gap is ~10x.
asi.root_cause_unknown: Full pipeline matvec performance is far worse than isolation. Need profiling with nsys/ncu to identify exact bottleneck.

description: Multi-row Q4_K matvec (4 rows/block) with shared Q8_1 cache. 24.5→23.6 tok/s (-3.7%). Shared memory overhead exceeds benefit.

asi.hypothesis: Multi-row Q4_K matvec (4 rows/block) with shared Q8_1 cache reduces launch overhead for large outputs (rc >= 4096)
asi.microbenchmark: Earlier microbenchmark showed multi-row was slightly slower in isolation
asi.reason: Shared memory load + extra __syncthreads() add overhead. Single-row kernel with 128 threads is already optimal for our workloads.
asi.revert: Remove k_q4k_mv_q8_dp4a_x4 and use original single-row kernel
asi.shared_mem: 6912 bytes per block for rb=24 - adds sync cost without benefit since L2 already caches Q8_1

description: GPU top-k for sampling: naive sequential block reduction. 24.6→12.2 tok/s (-50%). Need parallel heap-based approach.

asi.correctness: Output matched but kernel was too slow
asi.gpu_topk: Naive sequential top-k: 64 iterations of block reduction with O(K) exclusion per thread
asi.hypothesis: GPU top-k avoids 1MB D2H copy in sampling mode, saving ~1-2ms per token
asi.improvement_needed: Need parallel top-k (block-level heaps + merge) instead of sequential reduction
asi.reason: O(K*N) comparisons with thread divergence. 512 threads × 485 elems × 64 iterations = 15.9M comparisons with branches.
asi.topk_kernel: k_topk<<<1,512>>> with __syncthreads() per iteration - single block is bottleneck
